ann_op_table_investigation.next_action: Keep Q2 focused on the 0x700301d8/0x70030a0c command parser and nearby pointer/code tables; do not treat ANN op-name indices as the wrapper 10001..10009 dispatch mapping without a code reference.

elf_verification_1234.topic: INFO14/INFO15 settings tuple and settings+0x08 output fill

elf_verification_1234.decision: INFO12/INFO13 are closed at the ABI/provider plus 0x40-record layout level. ELF refs identify the record walk and a high-field validator island, but they do not yet prove every validated field is the same field later consumed by output/DMA use.

evidence.count_status: INFO12 count is closed at the ABI/kernel/provider boundary: firmware receives buffer_count, and the provider gates it below 0x21. After FLIX correction there is no byte-aligned LOOP/LOOPNEZ/LOOPGTZ to 0x7003c102; the loop_target body exposes stack-spill core ops plus FLIX bundles. A firmware-local count register name would require FLIX/TIE slot semantics, but the primitive model no longer depends on that name.
evidence.label: flix_assisted_INFO13_record_lead
evidence.stride_0x40_status: INFO13 stride is closed at the record-layout level: boundary-visible a2 descriptor loads cover offsets 0x2, 0x3, 0xa, 0xb, 0xe, 0xf, 0x12, 0x15, 0x29, 0x2c, 0x2d, 0x30, 0x31, 0x34, 0x35, 0x38, 0x39, 0x3c, 0x3d within a 0x40-byte record, and the kernel/provider copies INFO13 as struct vpu_buffer[INFO12] with 0x40 stride. The length-only FLIX overlay does not expose a boundary-visible a2 += 0x40 instruction in this owner.

evidence.count_status: byte-aligned hardware LOOP is present for this downgraded local control-flow target, but it is not the INFO13 descriptor walk.
evidence.label: downgraded_error_tail_loop_target

negative_evidence: 0x7003d423 remains downgraded local control-flow evidence and is not the INFO13 record walk
the validator island at 0x7003ce3c is field-validation evidence, not a full DMA-use pairing proof
elf_verification_1234.next_action: Treat INFO12/INFO13 and record stride as closed for this stage. Only reopen validation/use pairing if a concrete exploit argument needs a specific plane field to be paired with a later DMA use site.
elf_verification_1234.topic: INFO12/INFO13 descriptor count, 0x40 record layout, and validation/use pairing

elf_verification_1234.decision: The top iDMA schedule/wait owner is closed as an ELF anchor. The static ELF does not prove whether completion/output stores are single-burst or sequenced after a host-visible state change.

flix_length_rule_validation.assessment: The adopted FLIX length rule tiles every .xt.prop INSN run exactly. This turns the 0xe and 0xf op0 nibbles into 128-bit and 64-bit bundle widths instead of stock Xtensa 2-byte density items.
flix_length_rule_validation.next_action: Use this rule as the authoritative boundary layer for FLIX-heavy owners; slot mnemonics still require MVPU6F TIE/FLIX configuration or dynamic correlation.

flix_sweeps.description: Length-correct sweep across the 0x7003c102 loop-target neighborhood. Core LSAI-shaped accesses are interleaved with FLIX128/64 bundles instead of being swallowed by 2-byte base-Xtensa sizing.
flix_sweeps.label: info13_record_lead_corrected_boundaries
flix_sweeps.next_action: Use these boundaries before inspecting descriptor-field core ops. The 06 04 02 bytes are FLIX128 framing tails, not an independent selector.

flix_sweeps.description: Length-correct sweep around the downgraded 0x7003d423 loop-target property. The target itself is a core24 item, but the surrounding block remains switch/error-tail shaped.
flix_sweeps.label: downgraded_error_tail_corrected_boundaries
flix_sweeps.next_action: Keep as secondary local-control-flow evidence; corrected boundaries do not promote it back into the INFO13 mainline.

flix_sweeps.description: Length-correct sweep at the top iDMA schedule/wait owner. The owner starts with a standard entry core op followed by dense FLIX128/64 bundles and sparse core/density items.
flix_sweeps.label: dmaif_owner_entry_prefix_corrected_boundaries
flix_sweeps.next_action: Use this as the Q1 owner boundary map before any FLIX/iDMA instrumentation; string ownership alone still does not prove completion-store timing.

flix_sweeps.description: Length-correct sweep around the DRAM data-buffer validation string owner tail inside the same iDMA cluster.
flix_sweeps.label: dmaif_dram_validation_tail_corrected_boundaries
flix_sweeps.next_action: Correlate these core24 checks with the DRAM validation strings before treating the FLIX bundles as DMA movement.

focused_loop_investigations.assessment: Clean 0xaa-byte loop_target property run inside the strongest 0x40-record-shaped field cluster. FLIX-correct boundaries show the owner reads descriptor-shaped a2 fields within 0x02..0x3d; the 0x7003c102 loop body itself contains stack spills plus FLIX bundles. The length-only FLIX overlay therefore closes the record-layout evidence but cannot name a bundle-interior count register.
focused_loop_investigations.count_status: INFO12 count is closed at the ABI/kernel/provider boundary: firmware receives buffer_count, and the provider gates it below 0x21. After FLIX correction there is no byte-aligned LOOP/LOOPNEZ/LOOPGTZ to 0x7003c102; the loop_target body exposes stack-spill core ops plus FLIX bundles. A firmware-local count register name would require FLIX/TIE slot semantics, but the primitive model no longer depends on that name.
focused_loop_investigations.label: flix_assisted_INFO13_record_lead
focused_loop_investigations.next_action: Keep INFO12/INFO13 closed at the ABI plus record-layout level. Decode FLIX/TIE slots only if firmware-local count-register naming becomes necessary.

standard_islands.label: elf_entry_context_pack
standard_islands.note: Stable standard Xtensa island inside the 0x70006794 INFO16 entry; copies preload/context fields into the a10 scratch/context object.

standard_islands.label: early_helper_dispatch
standard_islands.note: Small helper that forwards a context-derived pointer to 0x70007440 and returns 0.

instructions.effect: skip indirect call if function pointer is null
call function pointer again in the timed dispatch path
call local helper in the return path
move saved argument/result into a10 before callback

standard_islands.label: early_dynamic_dispatch_standard_island
standard_islands.note: Standard instructions visible inside extension-heavy 0x70007440; shows ccount timing and repeated indirect calls through *(a12+0).

instructions.effect: load field from returned object at a2+0x0c
load field from returned object at a2+0x00
load field from a15+0x3c before callback
clamp callback result to at most 2
save clamped callback result on stack
store clamped callback result to a4+0x00

standard_islands.label: early_dynamic_dispatch_callback_loop
standard_islands.note: Second standard-instruction island inside the 0x70007440 owner. It confirms the early dynamic dispatcher has a repeated callback/polling loop after the first return-shaped island, not just one function-pointer call.

instructions.effect: load dispatch table/context field from a8+0x2c
move callback return value into a2

standard_islands.label: dispatcher_locateBuffer_trampoline
standard_islands.note: Byte-verified standard island at the dispatcher-like locateBuffer candidate. The l32r at 0x700301e3 loads the rodata suffix `locateBuffer` from 0x70001884, then the island reaches a branch toward the 0x70030240/0x70030a0c owner and an indirect call.

instructions.effect: skip first high-byte load when a3 is nonzero
shift high byte into a 16-bit field
dispatch based on decoded operand field
alternate jump into deeper operand parser
load table/context field before alternate parser
store decoded field to output/result slot +0x00
store decoded field to output/result slot +0x04
store decoded field to output/result slot +0x08
store decoded field to output/result slot +0x0c
store decoded field to output/result slot +0x10
shift combined field for later use
shift combined field for later use

standard_islands.label: dispatcher_operand_record_field_decode
standard_islands.note: Byte-verified standard instructions reached from the locateBuffer trampoline landing path. They read byte/halfword-shaped fields from the a2 record at offsets matching the DSP command operation/operand area, including +0x49/+0x4a near the first operand slot, then test the extracted 4-bit value against 1, 5, 6, and 9. This ties the 0x700301d8 dispatcher path to command/operand parsing; it is not yet the native INFO13 vpu_buffer-array parser.

instructions.effect: align stack pointer down to 64 bytes
load high byte field from a2+0x3a
load high byte field from a2+0x3b

standard_islands.label: buffer_record_high_field_validator_candidate
standard_islands.note: Byte-verified standard-instruction island in the 0x7003ce3c owner. It performs null/zero checks on an a2-based record using offsets +0x08, +0x0c, +0x10, +0x1c, +0x20, +0x24, +0x28, +0x34, and +0x38, plus byte fields at +0x39/+0x3a/+0x3b. These offsets overlap the high half of a 0x40-byte VPU buffer-shaped record, including plane length/pointer fields, but this island by itself does not prove the INFO12/INFO13 array loop or its iteration bound.
